sentences.sentence: Some of the most important decisions of our lives occur while we’re feeling stressed and anxious.
sentences.translation: 我们一生中一些最重要的决定，是在感到紧张和焦虑时作出的。

structure.text: Some of the most important decisions of our lives
while we’re feeling stressed and anxious

difficult_points: Some of the most important decisions of our lives 是较长的主语，两个 of 短语层层限定 decisions。
while 引导时间状语从句，说明这些重要决定发生时的心理状态。

expressions.text: the most important decisions of our lives
expressions.meaning_zh: 我们一生中最重要的决定

sentences.sentence: From medical decisions to financial and professional ones, we are all sometimes required to weigh up information under stressful conditions.
sentences.translation: 从医疗决定到财务和职业决定，我们有时都需要在有压力的情况下权衡信息。

structure.text: From medical decisions to financial and professional ones

difficult_points: ones 代替前面的 decisions，避免重复，指财务和职业方面的决定。
are required to 是被动结构，表示“被要求、需要去做某事”。

sentences.sentence: But do we become better or worse at processing and using information under such circumstances?
sentences.translation: 但在这种情况下，我们处理和使用信息的能力会变得更好还是更差呢？

structure.text: do we become better or worse

difficult_points: 疑问句中助动词 do 提前，主语 we 和谓语 become 保持原形。
processing and using information 是两个并列动名词，共同作介词 at 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: My colleague and I, both neuroscientists, wanted to investigate how the mind operates under stress, so we visited some local fire stations.
sentences.translation: 我的同事和我都是神经科学家，我们想研究大脑在压力下如何运作，于是走访了当地的一些消防站。

structure.text: how the mind operates under stress
so we visited some local fire stations

difficult_points: both neuroscientists 是插入式同位语，补充说明 My colleague and I 的身份。
how the mind operates under stress 是宾语从句，作 investigate 的宾语。
so 连接前后两个分句，表示研究目的导致了走访消防站这一行动。

sentences.sentence: Firefighters’ workdays vary quite a bit.
sentences.translation: 消防员的工作日差异很大。
difficult_points: Firefighters’ 是所有格形式，表示“消防员的”。
quite a bit 修饰 vary，表示变化幅度相当大。

sentences.sentence: Some are pretty relaxed; they’ll spend their time washing the truck, cleaning equipment, cooking meals and reading.
sentences.translation: 有些工作日相当轻松；消防员会花时间洗车、清洁设备、做饭和阅读。

structure.text: washing the truck, cleaning equipment, cooking meals and reading

difficult_points: Some 指代前一句中的 workdays，而不是 some firefighters。
分号连接两个关系紧密的分句，后半句具体说明轻松的工作日如何度过。
washing、cleaning、cooking 和 reading 并列，构成 spend time doing 的宾语补足内容。

sentences.sentence: Other days can be hectic, with numerous life-threatening incidents to attend to; they’ll enter burning homes to rescue trapped residents, and assist with medical emergencies.
sentences.translation: 另一些日子可能会忙乱不堪，有许多危及生命的事件需要处理；他们会进入燃烧的房屋营救被困居民，并协助处理医疗急救。

structure.text: with numerous life-threatening incidents to attend to

difficult_points: with numerous life-threatening incidents to attend to 补充说明 hectic 的原因。
to attend to 是后置不定式，修饰 incidents，表示“需要处理的事件”。
分号后列举消防员在繁忙日子里的具体行动。

sentences.sentence: These ups and downs presented the perfect setting for an experiment on how people’s ability to use information changes when they feel under pressure.
sentences.translation: 这些起伏变化为一项实验提供了理想环境，用来研究人们在感到压力时使用信息的能力会如何变化。

structure.text: for an experiment on how people’s ability to use information changes when they feel under pressure
how people’s ability to use information changes

difficult_points: These ups and downs 指前文消防员工作日轻松与紧张的波动。
how 引导的从句作介词 on 的宾语，说明实验关注的问题。
to use information 是不定式，后置修饰 people’s ability。

sentences.sentence: We found that perceived threat acted as a trigger for a stress reaction that made the task of processing information easier for the firefighters – but only as long as it conveyed bad news.
sentences.translation: 我们发现，感知到的威胁会触发压力反应，使消防员处理信息的任务变得更容易——但前提只是这些信息传递的是坏消息。

structure.text: that perceived threat acted as a trigger for a stress reaction
that made the task of processing information easier for the firefighters
but only as long as it conveyed bad news
as long as it conveyed bad news

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 found 的宾语从句，说明实验发现的内容。
第二个 that 引导定语从句，修饰 a stress reaction。
破折号后的 but only as long as 限定了这种效果只在信息为坏消息时出现。
it 指前文的信息内容，而不是 stress reaction。

sentences.sentence: This is how we arrived at these results.
sentences.translation: 我们就是这样得出这些结果的。

structure.text: how we arrived at these results

difficult_points: This 回指上一段的研究发现，引出接下来对实验方法的解释。
how 引导表语从句，说明“得出这些结果的方式”。

sentences.sentence: We asked the firefighters to estimate their likelihood of experiencing 40 different adverse events in their life, such as being involved in an accident or becoming a victim of card fraud.
sentences.translation: 我们让消防员估计自己一生中经历 40 种不同不利事件的可能性，例如卷入事故或成为银行卡诈骗的受害者。

structure.text: to estimate their likelihood of experiencing 40 different adverse events in their life
of experiencing 40 different adverse events in their life
such as being involved in an accident or becoming a victim of card fraud

difficult_points: ask someone to do something 结构中，to estimate 说明被要求完成的动作。
likelihood of experiencing 使用 of 加动名词，表示“经历……的可能性”。
such as 后面列出 adverse events 的具体例子。

sentences.sentence: We then gave them either good news (that their likelihood of experiencing these events was lower than they’d thought) or bad news (that it was higher) and asked them to provide new estimates.
sentences.translation: 然后我们告诉他们好消息（他们经历这些事件的可能性低于他们原先的估计）或坏消息（可能性更高），并要求他们给出新的估计。

structure.text: We then gave them either good news
that their likelihood of experiencing these events was lower than they’d thought
and asked them to provide new estimates

difficult_points: either ... or ... 连接 good news 与 bad news，构成二选一的信息反馈。
括号中的两个 that 从句分别解释 good news 和 bad news 的具体内容。
they’d thought 是 they had thought 的缩写，表示“他们先前以为”。

expressions.text: either good news or bad news
expressions.meaning_zh: 要么是好消息，要么是坏消息

sentences.sentence: People are normally quite optimistic – they will ignore bad news and embrace the good.
sentences.translation: 人们通常相当乐观——他们会忽视坏消息，接受好消息。

structure.text: they will ignore bad news and embrace the good
ignore bad news and embrace the good

difficult_points: 破折号后面的分句解释 optimistic 的具体表现。
the good 是“the + 形容词”结构，指好消息或好的方面。

sentences.sentence: This is what happened when the firefighters were relaxed; but when they were under stress, a different pattern emerged.
sentences.translation: 消防员放松时，情况正是如此；但当他们处于压力下时，出现了另一种模式。

structure.text: but when they were under stress, a different pattern emerged

difficult_points: what happened 是表语从句，说明 This 的内容。
分号后用 but 转折，对比放松状态和压力状态下的不同表现。
emerged 表示某种模式“出现、显现”，常用于研究结果描述。

sentences.sentence: Under these conditions, they became hyper-vigilant to bad news, even when it had nothing to do with their job (such as learning that the likelihood of card fraud was higher than they’d thought), and altered their beliefs in response.
sentences.translation: 在这些条件下，他们对坏消息变得高度警觉，即使这些坏消息与他们的工作毫无关系（例如得知银行卡诈骗的可能性高于他们原先的估计），他们也会相应改变自己的看法。

structure.text: they became hyper-vigilant to bad news
even when it had nothing to do with their job
such as learning that the likelihood of card fraud was higher than they’d thought
and altered their beliefs in response

difficult_points: Under these conditions 回指消防员处于压力状态这一条件。
it 指代前面的 bad news，即这些坏消息可能与工作无关。
such as learning... 用括号补充说明“与工作无关的坏消息”的例子。
altered their beliefs 与 became hyper-vigilant 并列，共同说明压力下的反应。

sentences.sentence: In contrast, stress didn’t change how they responded to good news (such as learning that the likelihood of card fraud was lower than they’d thought).
sentences.translation: 相比之下，压力并没有改变他们对好消息的反应方式（例如得知银行卡诈骗的可能性低于他们原先的估计）。

structure.text: how they responded to good news
such as learning that the likelihood of card fraud was lower than they’d thought
that the likelihood of card fraud was lower than they’d thought

difficult_points: In contrast 明确提示本句与上一句关于坏消息的反应形成对比。
how they responded to good news 是宾语从句，作 change 的宾语。
括号中的 such as learning... 举例说明 good news 的具体类型。

sentences.sentence: Back in our lab, we observed the same pattern in students who were told they had to give a surprise public speech, which would be judged by a panel, recorded and posted online.
sentences.translation: 回到实验室后，我们在学生身上观察到了同样的模式：他们被告知必须临时作一次公开演讲，而这场演讲会由评审小组评判、被录制并发布到网上。

structure.text: we observed the same pattern in students
who were told they had to give a surprise public speech
they had to give a surprise public speech
which would be judged by a panel, recorded and posted online

difficult_points: who 引导定语从句，修饰 students，说明这些学生被告知要演讲。
which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 public speech 会被如何处理。
judged、recorded 和 posted 是并列的被动动作。

sentences.sentence: Sure enough, their cortisol levels spiked, their heart rates went up and they suddenly became better at processing unrelated, yet alarming, information about rates of disease and violence.
sentences.translation: 果然，他们的皮质醇水平飙升，心率上升，并且他们突然更善于处理与演讲无关但令人警觉的疾病和暴力发生率信息。

structure.text: at processing unrelated, yet alarming, information about rates of disease and violence

difficult_points: Sure enough 表示结果符合预期，承接上一句的实验设置。
spiked、went up 和 became better 构成三个并列结果。
unrelated, yet alarming 用 yet 表示“虽然无关但令人担忧”的转折。

sentences.sentence: When we experience stressful events, a physiological change is triggered that causes us to take in warnings and focus on what might go wrong.
sentences.translation: 当我们经历有压力的事件时，一种生理变化会被触发，使我们吸收警告信息并关注可能出错的地方。

structure.text: that causes us to take in warnings and focus on what might go wrong
to take in warnings and focus on what might go wrong

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明生理变化被触发的条件。
is triggered 是被动语态，强调生理变化被某种事件激活。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 a physiological change，并说明其结果。

sentences.sentence: Brain imaging reveals that this ‘switch’ is related to a sudden boost in a neural signal important for learning, specifically in response to unexpected warning signs, such as faces expressing fear.
sentences.translation: 脑成像显示，这个“开关”与一种对学习很重要的神经信号突然增强有关，尤其是对意外警告信号的反应，比如表现出恐惧的面孔。

structure.text: that this ‘switch’ is related to a sudden boost in a neural signal important for learning
specifically in response to unexpected warning signs

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明脑成像揭示的具体内容。
important for learning 是后置形容词短语，修饰 a neural signal。
such as faces expressing fear 举例说明 unexpected warning signs。

sentences.sentence: Such neural engineering could have helped prehistoric humans to survive.
sentences.translation: 这种神经机制可能曾帮助史前人类生存下来。
difficult_points: Such neural engineering 回指上一段提到的神经“开关”机制。
could have helped 表示对过去可能性的推测，而不是确定事实。

sentences.sentence: When our ancestors found themselves surrounded by hungry animals, they would have benefited from an increased ability to learn about hazards.
sentences.translation: 当我们的祖先发现自己被饥饿的动物包围时，他们会因学习危险信息的能力增强而受益。

structure.text: When our ancestors found themselves surrounded by hungry animals
found themselves surrounded by hungry animals
from an increased ability to learn about hazards

difficult_points: found themselves surrounded by... 是“发现自己处于某种状态”的结构。
surrounded by hungry animals 是过去分词短语，说明 themselves 的处境。
would have benefited 表示对过去情境的推测性结果。

sentences.sentence: In a safe environment, however, it would have been wasteful to be on high alert constantly.
sentences.translation: 然而，在安全环境中，一直保持高度警觉本会是一种浪费。

structure.text: to be on high alert constantly

difficult_points: however 插入句中，表示与前一句危险环境形成转折。
it 是形式主语，真正的主语是不定式 to be on high alert constantly。
would have been 表示对过去假设情况的判断。

sentences.sentence: So, a neural switch that automatically increases or decreases our ability to process warnings in response to changes in our environment could have been useful.
sentences.translation: 因此，一个能根据环境变化自动增强或降低我们处理警告信息能力的神经开关，可能曾经很有用。

structure.text: that automatically increases or decreases our ability to process warnings
in response to changes in our environment

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 a neural switch，说明这个开关的功能。
increases or decreases 是并列谓语，表示能力可上调也可下调。
could have been useful 表示这种机制在过去可能具有适应价值。

sentences.sentence: In fact, people with clinical depression and anxiety seem unable to switch away from a state in which they absorb all the negative messages around them.
sentences.translation: 事实上，患有临床抑郁和焦虑的人似乎无法摆脱一种会吸收周围所有负面信息的状态。

structure.text: people with clinical depression and anxiety
seem unable to switch away from a state
in which they absorb all the negative messages around them
all the negative messages around them

difficult_points: with clinical depression and anxiety 是介词短语，限定 people 的范围。
in which 引导定语从句，修饰 a state，相当于 in a state where。
switch away from 表示从某种状态中切换离开。

sentences.sentence: It is also important to realise that stress travels rapidly from one person to the next.
sentences.translation: 同样重要的是要认识到，压力会迅速从一个人传到另一个人。

structure.text: that stress travels rapidly from one person to the next
from one person to the next

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正要表达的内容由 to realise 引出。
that 引导 realise 的宾语从句，说明需要认识到的事实。
from one person to the next 表示压力在人与人之间传递。

expressions.text: from one person to the next
expressions.meaning_zh: 从一个人传到另一个人

sentences.sentence: If a co-worker is stressed, we are more likely to tense up and feel stressed ourselves.
sentences.translation: 如果同事感到压力，我们自己也更有可能紧张起来并感到压力。

structure.text: to tense up and feel stressed ourselves

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明压力传染发生的条件。
are more likely to 表示“更有可能做某事”。
tense up 和 feel stressed 是并列动作，描述受到他人压力影响后的反应。

sentences.sentence: We don’t even need to be in the same room with someone for their emotions to influence our behaviour.
sentences.translation: 我们甚至不需要和某人在同一个房间里，他们的情绪也能影响我们的行为。

structure.text: to be in the same room with someone
for their emotions to influence our behaviour

difficult_points: don’t even need 强调“甚至不需要”，突出情绪影响不依赖近距离接触。
for their emotions to influence our behaviour 是 for + 名词 + 不定式结构，表示结果或发生条件。
influence our behaviour 表明被影响的是行为，而不仅是情绪感受。

sentences.sentence: Studies show that if we observe positive feeds on social media, such as images of a pink sunset, we are more likely to post uplifting messages ourselves.
sentences.translation: 研究表明，如果我们在社交媒体上看到积极的信息流，比如粉色日落的图片，我们自己也更有可能发布鼓舞人心的信息。

structure.text: that if we observe positive feeds on social media, such as images of a pink sunset, we are more likely to post uplifting messages ourselves
if we observe positive feeds on social media
such as images of a pink sunset
we are more likely to post uplifting messages ourselves

difficult_points: that 引导 show 的宾语从句，整个从句说明研究发现。
宾语从句内部又包含 if 条件从句，构成“如果看到积极内容，就更可能发布积极内容”的逻辑。
such as images of a pink sunset 举例说明 positive feeds。

sentences.sentence: If we observe negative posts, such as complaints about a long queue at the coffee shop, we will in turn create more negative posts.
sentences.translation: 如果我们看到负面帖子，比如抱怨咖啡店长队的内容，我们又会相应发布更多负面帖子。

structure.text: such as complaints about a long queue at the coffee shop
we will in turn create more negative posts

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，说明产生更多负面帖子的条件。
such as 后的 complaints about... 是 negative posts 的例子。
in turn 表示“反过来、继而”，强调负面内容会被继续传播。

sentences.sentence: In some ways, many of us now live as if we are in danger, constantly ready to tackle demanding emails and text messages, and respond to news alerts and comments on social media.
sentences.translation: 在某些方面，我们许多人如今生活得仿佛身处危险之中，时刻准备处理高强度的电子邮件和短信，并回应社交媒体上的新闻提醒和评论。

structure.text: as if we are in danger
constantly ready to tackle demanding emails and text messages, and respond to news alerts and comments on social media
to tackle demanding emails and text messages, and respond to news alerts and comments on social media

difficult_points: as if 引导方式状语从句，表示“仿佛处在危险中”。
constantly ready... 是形容词短语，补充说明 many of us 的持续状态。
tackle 和 respond to 并列，说明现代人不断处理的信息任务。

sentences.sentence: Repeatedly checking your phone, according to a survey conducted by the American Psychological Association, is related to stress.
sentences.translation: 根据美国心理学会的一项调查，反复查看手机与压力有关。

structure.text: according to a survey conducted by the American Psychological Association
conducted by the American Psychological Association

difficult_points: Repeatedly checking your phone 是动名词短语作主语，谓语用单数 is。
according to... 是插入语，说明信息来源。
conducted by... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 a survey。

sentences.sentence: In other words, a pre-programmed physiological reaction, which evolution has equipped us with to help us avoid famished predators, is now being triggered by an online post.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，一种由进化赋予我们、帮助我们躲避饥饿捕食者的预设生理反应，如今正被一条网络帖子触发。

structure.text: which evolution has equipped us with to help us avoid famished predators
to help us avoid famished predators
is now being triggered by an online post

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明这种生理反应的进化功能。
has equipped us with 表示进化“赋予我们”这种反应。
is now being triggered 是现在进行时的被动语态，强调现代触发源正在发生变化。

sentences.sentence: Social media posting, according to one study, raises your pulse, makes you sweat, and enlarges your pupils more than most daily activities.
sentences.translation: 根据一项研究，社交媒体发帖比大多数日常活动更能让你脉搏加快、出汗并瞳孔放大。

structure.text: raises your pulse, makes you sweat, and enlarges your pupils

difficult_points: according to one study 是插入语，说明该说法的研究来源。
raises、makes 和 enlarges 三个动词并列，共同说明发帖带来的生理反应。
more than most daily activities 将社交媒体发帖与大多数日常活动作比较。

sentences.sentence: The fact that stress increases the likelihood that we will focus more on alarming messages, together with the fact that it spreads extremely rapidly, can create collective fear that is not always justified.
sentences.translation: 压力会增加我们更多关注警示性信息的可能性，再加上它传播极快，这两个事实会造成并不总是有充分理由的集体恐惧。

structure.text: The fact that stress increases the likelihood that we will focus more on alarming messages
that we will focus more on alarming messages
together with the fact that it spreads extremely rapidly

difficult_points: 主语是 The fact... together with the fact...，由两个事实共同构成，结构较长。
句中有多层 that：前两个解释 fact 或 likelihood，最后一个修饰 collective fear。
it 指代 stress，说明压力不仅影响注意力，还会快速传播。
not always justified 表示这种集体恐惧并非总有充分依据。

sentences.sentence: After a stressful public event, such as a natural disaster or major financial crash, there is often a wave of alarming information in traditional and social media, which individuals become very aware of.
sentences.translation: 在一次有压力的公共事件之后，例如自然灾害或重大金融崩盘，传统媒体和社交媒体中常会出现一波令人警觉的信息，而个人会强烈注意到这些信息。

structure.text: such as a natural disaster or major financial crash
there is often a wave of alarming information
which individuals become very aware of

difficult_points: After a stressful public event 是时间背景，such as 后给出具体例子。
there is 是存在句，主语概念是后面的 a wave of alarming information。
which individuals become very aware of 是定语从句，of 保留在句末，指向 alarming information。

sentences.sentence: But that has the effect of exaggerating existing danger.
sentences.translation: 但这会产生夸大现有危险的效果。
difficult_points: that 回指上一句中人们对大量警示信息高度关注这件事。
has the effect of 后接动名词，表示“产生……的效果”。

sentences.sentence: And so, a reliable pattern emerges – stress is triggered, spreading from one person to the next, which temporarily enhances the likelihood that people will take in negative reports, which increases stress further.
sentences.translation: 于是，一个可靠的模式出现了——压力被触发，从一个人传播到另一个人，这会暂时提高人们吸收负面报道的可能性，而这又进一步增加压力。

structure.text: spreading from one person to the next
which temporarily enhances the likelihood that people will take in negative reports

difficult_points: 破折号后具体解释 a reliable pattern 的内容。
spreading from one person to the next 是现在分词短语，说明 stress 被触发后的传播方式。
第一个 which 指代压力传播这一情况，并引出其对吸收负面报道的影响。
第二个 which 指代吸收负面报道增多这一情况，说明压力进一步增加。

sentences.sentence: As a result, trips are cancelled, even if the disaster took place across the globe; stocks are sold, even when holding on is the best thing to do.
sentences.translation: 结果，即使灾难发生在地球另一端，旅行也会被取消；即使持有股票才是最好的做法，人们也会抛售股票。

structure.text: even if the disaster took place across the globe
even when holding on is the best thing to do

difficult_points: trips are cancelled 和 stocks are sold 都是被动语态，强调结果而非行动者。
even if 与 even when 引出让步条件，说明反应可能与实际最佳选择不符。
holding on 是动名词短语作主语，指继续持有股票。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在全球另一端；遍及全球

sentences.sentence: The good news, however, is that positive emotions, such as hope, are contagious too, and are powerful in inducing people to act to find solutions.
sentences.translation: 然而，好消息是，积极情绪，例如希望，也具有传染性，并且能有力地促使人们采取行动寻找解决方案。

structure.text: that positive emotions, such as hope, are contagious too
and are powerful in inducing people to act to find solutions

difficult_points: however 插入主语和谓语之间，表示本段从负面传播转向积极情绪。
that 引导表语从句，说明 The good news 的具体内容。
inducing people to act to find solutions 中有连续不定式，表示促使人们行动并寻找方案。

sentences.sentence: Being aware of the close relationship between people’s emotional state and how they process information can help us frame our messages more effectively and become conscientious agents of change.
sentences.translation: 意识到人们的情绪状态与其处理信息方式之间的密切关系，可以帮助我们更有效地组织信息，并成为有责任感的变革推动者。

structure.text: Being aware of the close relationship between people’s emotional state and how they process information
between people’s emotional state and how they process information
frame our messages more effectively and become conscientious agents of change

difficult_points: Being aware of... 是动名词短语作主语，整段内容视为一个概念。
between 连接 people’s emotional state 和 how they process information，后一项是名词性从句。
help us 后省略 to，直接接 frame 和 become 两个并列动词。

expressions.meaning_zh: 组织、设计我们的信息表达
